page: Amazon Web Services x8aedz.6xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9R05 processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 24 dedicated physical cores operating at a clock speed of 5.0 GHz, with one thread per core. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 19 Gbps. The instance runs on the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple vectors. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance, evaluated via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls within the top 10% of tested instances. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is also in the top 10%, reflecting the high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory architecture. In cryptographic and compression tests, such as single-threaded gzip and bzip3 decompression, the server achieves top-tier results. Large-model LLM inference shows a tradeoff, with prompt processing speed for a 70B model ranking in the middle 50% while text generation remains in the top 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high resource density of 768.0 GB RAM combined with dedicated 5.0 GHz cores and local NVMe storage provides a consolidated hardware profile that reduces the need for multi-node clustering for memory-heavy tasks. This server is configured for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, and software compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services x8aedz.6xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 24 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R05 processor running at 5.0 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 32.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes a 1900 GB local NVMe SSD. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. Benchmark results place its single-core CPU performance, large-block memory bandwidth, and image processing capabilities in the top 10% of tested servers. While LLM prompt processing for large 70B models falls in the middle 50%, text generation remains highly efficient. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and high-throughput caching systems.

tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring dedicated 5.0 GHz AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and high-speed local NVMe storage.

768.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory with 32.0 GB per core
